title: -Ratification of City Manager Action to Execute a Contract with Lake Research Partners for Community Survey Services in Support of Potential November 2026 Ballot Measures

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during Council recess
to execute a contract in an amount not to exceed $100,000 with Lake Research
Partners to develop and administer up to two community surveys of registered Berkeley
voters in support of potential ballot measures for the November 3, 2026, General
Municipal Election, with a contract term of January 5, 2026, through December 31,
2026.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
Delaying ratification of the contract until after Council recess would slow the City’s
ballot-measure development timeline by delaying survey design, fielding, and analysis.
Such delay would limit the City Council’s ability to receive survey results in time to
inform decisions regarding potential ballot measures.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The contract with Lake Research Partners has a not-to-exceed amount of $100,000 for
a contract term of January 5, 2026, through December 31, 2026. Funds are available
within existing appropriations in the General Fund. No additional appropriation is
required because of this action.
sections.current_situation:
The City is evaluating potential initiatives and revenue measures for possible placement
on the November 3, 2026, ballot. Consistent with prior ballot cycles, the City relies on
professionally administered community surveys to assess voter satisfaction with City
services, identify community priorities, and evaluate voter support for potential funding
mechanisms.
To maintain alignment with the City Council’s ballot-measure schedule and ensure
sufficient time for survey development and analysis, the City Manager executed a

contract during Council recess with Lake Research Partners, following a competitive
solicitation process, to conduct up to two community surveys.
This effort advances the City’s Strategic Plan goal to be a customer-focused
organization that provides excellent, timely, easily-accessible service and information to
the community, by ensuring major policy and funding decisions are informed by
transparent, data-driven public input.
sections.background:
The City Manager’s Office oversees periodic community surveys to inform City Council
consideration of potential ballot measures and broader policy priorities. For the
November 2026 election cycle, the City will initiate a community survey process to
assess voter satisfaction with City services, identify community investment priorities,
and evaluate voter support for potential funding mechanisms.
On November 18, 2025, the City Council authorized issuance of a Request for
Proposals (RFP) for the 2026 Community Survey (Specification No. 26-11772-C). The
RFP was issued to potential bidders on November 19, 2025, with questions due on
December 2, 2025, and proposals due on December 9, 2025.
The city received four completed proposals in response to the RFP. A city staff
evaluation panel reviewed the proposals and identified two (2) finalist firms to advance
to interviews. Finalist interviews were conducted during the week of December 15,
2025, and the selection process was completed on December 19, 2025.
Following the evaluation process, the City Manager selected Lake Research Partners
based on demonstrated understanding of the scope of services, proposed methodology,
relevant experience, qualifications of key staff, references, and overall value to the City.
To maintain alignment with the City Council’s ballot-measure development timeline and
ensure sufficient time for survey design, fielding, analysis, and presentation of results,
the City Manager executed a contract with Lake Research Partners during Council
recess.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
While this action does not directly fund or construct infrastructure, the community survey
will inform City Council decisions regarding potential investments that advance
Berkeley’s climate and sustainability goals, consistent with the Climate Action Plan and
Vision 2050 Framework. The survey will be conducted using electronic and/or telephone
methodologies, minimizing paper use and supporting the City’s envir
sections.rationale:
Ratifying the City Manager’s action ensures continuity in the City’s ballot-measure
planning process, preserves critical election timelines, and supports informed City
Council decision-making. Proceeding during Council recess was necessary to avoid

Page 3 of 4

January 20, 2026
delays that could compromise the City’s ability to engage voters and meet required
election deadlines.
sections.alternatives:
The City Council could decline to ratify the City Manager’s action, which would delay the
community survey process and risk limiting the City’s ability to develop informed and
viable ballot measures for the November 2026 election.
CONTACT PERSON
Carianna Arredondo, Assistant to the City Manager, City Manager’s Office, 510-981-
7000
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
RATIFYING ACTION TAKEN BY THE CITY MANAGER DURING COUNCIL RECESS
TO EXECUTE A CONTRACT WITH LAKE RESEARCH PARTNERS IN AN AMOUNT
NOT TO EXCEED $100,000 FOR COMMUNITY SURVEY SERVICES IN SUPPORT
OF POTENTIAL NOVEMBER 2026 BALLOT MEASURES
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley is evaluating potential initiatives and revenue
measures for possible placement on the November 3, 2026, General Municipal Election;
and
WHEREAS, professionally administered community surveys are an important tool to
assess voter satisfaction with City services, identify community priorities, and evaluate
voter support for potential funding mechanisms; and
WHEREAS, on November 18, 2025, the City Council authorized issuance of a Request
for Proposals for the 2026 Community Survey (Specification No. 26-11772-C); and
WHEREAS, following a competitive solicitation process, the City received four
proposals, interviewed two finalist firms, and selected Lake Research Partners based
on qualifications, experience, methodology, and overall value to the City; and
WHEREAS, to maintain alignment with the City Council’s ballot-measure development
timeline and meet statutory deadlines for the November 2026 election, the City Manager
executed a contract with Lake Research Partners during Council recess.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager to execute a contract with Lake
Research, in an amount not to exceed $100,000 to develop and administer up to two
community surveys of registered Berkeley voters in support of potential ballot measures
for the November 2026 General Municipal Election, with a contract term of January 5,
2026, through December 31, 2026.
